title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:1
body: By those that wrest by the angels who wrest the souls of disbelievers violently;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:10
body: They those of the mentioned hearts and eyes will say mockingly and in rejection of the Resurrection ‘Are we indeed read a-innā pronouncing both hamzas or by not pronouncing the second and inserting an alif between them in both cases and in both instances being restored as before? Shall we be restored to life after death al-hāfira is a noun signifying the ‘first part’ of anything from which is derived the expression raja‘a fī hāfiratihi to mean that so and so ‘returned whence he had come’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:11
body: What! When we have been decayed bones?’ nakhira a variant reading has nākhira ‘decayed and withered’ will we have life again?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:12
body: They will say ‘That return of ours to life then if it were true would be a ruinous return!’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:13
body: God exalted be He says But it the Aftershock which will be followed by the Resurrection will be only a single blast and so when it is blasted

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:14
body: behold then they all creatures will be upon the surface of the earth alive after having been dead inside it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:15
body: Have you O Muhammad (s) received the story of Moses hadīthu Mūsā operates the clause that follows

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:16
body: when his Lord called out to him in the holy valley of Tuwā? — this is the name of the valley it may be read with nunation Tuwan or without — and He said

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:17
body: ‘Go to Pharaoh; he has indeed become rebellious he has exceeded all bounds with his disbelief

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:18
body: and say “Would you — I call you to — purify yourself tazakkā a variant reading has tazzakkā where the original second tā’ of tatazakkā has been assimilated with the zāy to purge yourself of idolatry by bearing witness that there is no god but God

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:19
body: and allow me to guide you to your Lord to show you how to know Him ma‘rifa by way of proofs so that you may have fear of Him?” ’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:2
body: by those that draw out by the angels who draw out the souls of believers gently;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:20
body: So he showed him the greatest sign from among His nine signs namely the glowing hand or the slithering staff.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:21
body: But he Pharaoh denied Moses and disobeyed God exalted be He.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:22
body: Then he turned his back to faith going about in haste throughout the land causing corruption.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:23
body: Then he gathered he assembled the sorcerers and his armies and proclaimed

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:24
body: and said ‘I am your most high lord!’ above whom is no other lord.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:25
body: So God seized him He destroyed him by drowning him as punishment for the latter that is to say these last words of his and for the former that is to say his saying previously ‘I do not know of any god for you other than me’ Q. 2838 — and between the two claims made by Pharaoh was an interval of forty years.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:26
body: Assuredly in that which is mentioned there is a moral for him who fears God exalted be He.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:27
body: Are you read a-antum pronouncing both hamzas or by substituting an alif for the second one not pronouncing it and inserting an alif between the one not pronounced and the former or without this insertion namely you the deniers of the Resurrection harder to create or the heaven which He has built? harder to create banāhā an explication of the manner of its creation.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:28
body: He made it rise high — an explication of the manner of its construction; in other words He made its vertical extension high; it is also said that samkahā means ‘its ceiling’ — and levelled it He made it even flawless

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:29
body: and darkened its night and brought forth its day He exposed the light of its sun ‘night’ has been annexed to it the heaven because it represents its shade and likewise the ‘sun’ because it represents its light;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:3
body: by those that glide serenely by the angels who descend from the heavens with His command exalted be He;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:30
body: and after that He spread out the earth He made it flat for it had been created before the heaven but without having been spread out;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:31
body: from it He has brought forth akhraja a circumstantial qualifier with a suppressed preceding qad that is to say mukhrijan ‘bringing forth from it’ its waters by making its springs gush forth and its pastures what cattle graze of trees and herbage and what humans consume of foods and fruits the use of mar‘ā to express this of the earth is figurative

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:32
body: and has set firm the mountains on the face of the earth so that it stays still

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:33
body: as a source of sustenance an object denoting reason for an implied verbal clause in other words fa‘ala dhālika mut‘atan or tamtī‘an ‘He did this to provide a source of sustenance’ for you and your flocks an‘ām is the plural of na‘am which are camels cows and sheep.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:34
body: So when the Greatest Catastrophe befalls — the second blast —

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:35
body: the day when man will remember yawma yatadhakkaru’l-insānu substitutes for idhā ‘when’ his efforts in the life of the world in the way of good or evil

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:36
body: and the Hell-fire the consuming Fire is revealed for all to see for every on-looker the response to idhā ‘when’ is what follows

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:37
body: as for him who was rebellious who disbelieved

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:38
body: and preferred the life of this world by pursuing carnal desires

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:39
body: Hell-fire will indeed be the abode his abode.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:4
body: by those that race forward by the angels who race forward to Paradise with the souls of believers;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:40
body: But as for him who feared the stance before his Lord his standing before Him and forbade the evil-bidding soul from pursuing desire that leads to perdition as a result of that person’s lusting after carnal desires

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:41
body: Paradise will indeed be the abode in sum the response to idhā ‘when’ verse 34 is that the disobedient one will be in the Fire and the obedient one in Paradise.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:42
body: They that is the disbelievers of Mecca will ask you about the Hour when will it set in? when will it come to pass and when will it begin?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:43
body: What have you to do with the mention of it? in other words you have no knowledge of it in order to mention it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:44
body: With your Lord it belongs ultimately ultimate knowledge of it lies with Him; none other than Him has any knowledge of it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:45
body: You are only a warner for your warning will only benefit the one who fears it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:46
body: The day they see it it will be as if they had only tarried in their graves for an evening or the morning thereof that is to say as if only one evening of a day or its morning the annexation of duhā ‘morning’ to ‘ashiyya ‘evening’ is valid on the basis of their contiguity since both constitute either side of the day; the annexation has also allowed for the word to fall in harmony with the end-rhyme of the verses.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:5
body: and by those that direct the affair by the angels who direct the affairs of this world that is to say they descend with the directions for it from God the response to all of these oath clauses has been omitted understood to be something like la-tub‘athunna yā kuffāra Makka ‘you shall certainly be resurrected O disbelievers of Mecca!’ which is also the operator of the following clause

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:6
body: the day when the Tremor quakes that is to say the first blast as a result of which everything will be shaken violently thus it the subject has been described in terms of the effect it produces

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:7
body: and is followed by the Aftershock the second blast; between the two blasts is a span of forty years the sentence tatba‘uhā’l-rādifatu is a circumstantial qualifier referring to al-rājifa ‘the Tremor’ in other words the ‘day’ can adverbially accommodate both blasts and other events and so for this reason it can also properly function as the adverbial qualifier for the Resurrection that will take place after the second blast;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:8
body: on that day hearts will be trembling frightened and anxious

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 79:9
body: their eyes humbled abject because of the terror that they see.
